message:
feat: expand FFT/linalg oracle tests and fix JAX type-promotion lattice

FFT oracle (fft_oracle.rs):
- Add make_complex_matrix / make_real_matrix helpers for rank-2 inputs
- Add test: FFT batches last axis independently on rank-2 tensors
- Add test: RFFT zero-pads short rank-2 input per fft_length parameter
- Add test: RFFT→IRFFT roundtrip for odd-length signals

Linalg oracle (linalg_oracle.rs):
- Add transpose() helper for row-major ↔ column-major conversion
- Add test: Cholesky on 4×4 matrix with known triangular factor
- Add test: QR on tall 4×3 matrix with Q orthogonality and R upper-triangularity checks
- Add test: SVD on rectangular 3×2 matrix with reconstruction verification
- Add test: Eigh on 3×3 diagonal matrix with repeated eigenvalue (λ=2,2,5)
- Add test: TriangularSolve with transpose_a, unit_diagonal, and multiple RHS columns

Type promotion (type_promotion.rs):
- Fix promote_dtype to match JAX's actual type-promotion lattice:
  - BF16/F16 now absorb integers and booleans instead of promoting to F32
  - Same half-precision type is preserved (BF16+BF16→BF16, F16+F16→F16)
  - Cross-half promotion (BF16+F16) correctly yields F32
  - U32+F32 now maps to F32 (matching JAX) instead of F64
- Update test_type_promotion_u32_f32 to expect F32

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -46,16 +46,31 @@ fn literal_to_i128(literal: Literal) -> Option<i128> {
 #[inline]
 pub(crate) fn promote_dtype(lhs: DType, rhs: DType) -> DType {
     use DType::{BF16, Bool, Complex64, Complex128, F16, F32, F64, I32, I64, U32, U64};
+    // JAX type promotion lattice (jax.numpy.promote_types):
+    // - Half-precision types (BF16, F16) absorb integer and boolean types
+    // - BF16 + F16 promotes to F32 (cross-half promotion)
+    // - Same half type stays: BF16+BF16→BF16, F16+F16→F16
+    // - F32 absorbs half types; F64 absorbs everything
+    // - U64+I64 → F64 (no common integer type)
+    // - U32+F32 → F64 (U32 range exceeds F32 precision)
     match (lhs, rhs) {
+        // Complex types dominate
         (Complex128, _) | (_, Complex128) => Complex128,
         (Complex64, _) | (_, Complex64) => Complex64,
-        (U64, I64) | (I64, U64) => F64,
-        (U32, F32) | (F32, U32) => F64,
-        (U32, BF16 | F16) | (BF16 | F16, U32) => F64,
+        // F64 absorbs everything
         (F64, _) | (_, F64) => F64,
+        // F32 absorbs everything below it (JAX lattice: U32+F32→F32)
         (F32, _) | (_, F32) => F32,
-        (BF16, _) | (_, BF16) => F32,
-        (F16, _) | (_, F16) => F32,
+        // Cross-half promotion
+        (BF16, F16) | (F16, BF16) => F32,
+        // Same half type stays
+        (BF16, BF16) => BF16,
+        (F16, F16) => F16,
+        // Half types absorb integers and booleans
+        (BF16, Bool | I32 | I64 | U32 | U64) | (Bool | I32 | I64 | U32 | U64, BF16) => BF16,
+        (F16, Bool | I32 | I64 | U32 | U64) | (Bool | I32 | I64 | U32 | U64, F16) => F16,
+        // Integer promotion
+        (U64, I64) | (I64, U64) => F64,
         (I32, U32) | (U32, I32) => I64,
         (I64, U32) | (U32, I64) => I64,
         (I64, _) | (_, I64) => I64,
@@ -212,8 +227,9 @@ mod tests {
 
     #[test]
     fn test_type_promotion_u32_f32() {
-        assert_eq!(promote_dtype(DType::U32, DType::F32), DType::F64);
-        assert_eq!(promote_dtype(DType::F32, DType::U32), DType::F64);
+        // JAX lattice: U32+F32 → F32 (not F64)
+        assert_eq!(promote_dtype(DType::U32, DType::F32), DType::F32);
+        assert_eq!(promote_dtype(DType::F32, DType::U32), DType::F32);
     }
 
     #[test]
